message.content:
Study specs/006-user-interface.md thoroughly. Extract:
1. Infinite Canvas architecture (deck.gl, Konva, React DOM)
2. Semantic zoom levels and transitions
3. All UI components required
4. Drag-and-drop rescheduling requirements
5. State management approach
6. Accessibility requirements
Search src/ for existing UI components to identify gaps.

file.content:
# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 6: User Interface Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 6.1 Design System Overview

### 6.1.1 Technology Stack

| Component | Technology | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| Framework | React 18 | Component architecture |
| State | Zustand | Global state management |
| Canvas | deck.gl | WebGL timeline rendering |
| Components | BlueprintJS v6 | UI component library |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first styling |
| Build | Vite | Development and bundling |
| Types | TypeScript | Type safety |

### 6.1.2 Design Principles

1. **Information Density**: Maximize data visibility without overwhelming
2. **Semantic Zoom**: Data representation adapts to zoom level
3. **Direct Manipulation**: Drag-and-drop with immediate feedback
4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Details on demand
5. **Constraint Visibility**: Show limits, not just violations
6. **Explainability**: Every system decision has visible rationale

### 6.1.3 Color System

```typescript
const colors = {
  // PM Status
  pm: {
    scheduled: '#3B82F6',    // Blue
    inProgress: '#F59E0B',   // Amber
    completed: '#10B981',    // Green
    cancelled: '#6B7280',    // Gray
    deferred: '#8B5CF6',     // Purple
    overdue: '#EF4444',      // Red
  },
  
  // Constraint Indicators
  constraints: {
    hardViolation: '#DC2626',    // Red-600
    softViolation: '#F59E0B',    // Amber-500
    approaching: '#FBBF24',      // Yellow-400
    ok: '#10B981',               // Green-500
  },
  
  // Drift Severity
  drift: {
    critical: '#DC2626',     // Red
    warning: '#F59E0B',      // Amber
    ok: '#10B981',           // Green
  },
  
  // Utilization
  utilization: {
    overloaded: '#DC2626',   // Red
    optimal: '#10B981',      // Green
    underutilized: '#3B82F6', // Blue
  },
  
  // Fleet Colors
  fleets: {
    KS: '#0EA5E9',           // Sky-500
    ALSTOM: '#8B5CF6',       // Violet-500
  }
};
```

---

## 6.2 Application Layout

   224→### 6.3.5 Hybrid Rendering Architecture
   225→
   226→**CRITICAL:** The canvas uses **hybrid rendering** to optimize for each zoom level:
   227→
   228→| Zoom Level | Renderer | Rationale |
   229→|------------|----------|-----------|
   230→| Strategic (0-4) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Heatmap cells, no text needed |
   231→| Tactical-Wide (4-8) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Compressed blocks, minimal text |
   232→| Tactical (8-12) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Gantt bars with crisp text labels |
   233→| Operational (12-16) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Detailed cards with readable text |
   234→| Detail (16-20) | **React DOM** | Rich interactions, form inputs |
   235→
   236→**Why Hybrid?**
   237→
   238→deck.gl uses WebGL for rendering, which has limitations for text-heavy views:
   239→- Font atlases blur during zoom operations
   240→- Hit-testing for drag-and-drop is complex in WebGL
   241→- Text rendering feels less crisp than native canvas/DOM
   242→
   243→By switching to Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D) at tactical zoom levels, we get:
   244→- Crisp text rendering at all scales
   245→- Native canvas hit-testing for smooth drag-and-drop
   246→- Better performance for hundreds of labeled PM blocks
   247→
   248→```typescript

## 6.3 Infinite Canvas Component

### 6.3.1 Zoom Levels and Data Representation

| Zoom Level | Time Span | Granularity | Visual Representation |
|------------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|
| Strategic | 12 years | Yearly | Heatmap cells (PM density) |
| Tactical-Wide | 1 year | Monthly | Compressed Gantt blocks |
| Tactical | 12 weeks | Weekly | Full Gantt blocks with labels |
| Operational | 4 weeks | Daily | Detailed cards with all info |
| Detail | 1 week | Daily | Expanded cards with crew assignment |

### 6.3.5 Hybrid Rendering Architecture

**CRITICAL:** The canvas uses **hybrid rendering** to optimize for each zoom level:

| Zoom Level | Renderer | Rationale |
|------------|----------|-----------|
| Strategic (0-4) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Heatmap cells, no text needed |
| Tactical-Wide (4-8) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Compressed blocks, minimal text |
| Tactical (8-12) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Gantt bars with crisp text labels |
| Operational (12-16) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Detailed cards with readable text |
| Detail (16-20) | **React DOM** | Rich interactions, form inputs |

**Why Hybrid?**

deck.gl uses WebGL for rendering, which has limitations for text-heavy views:
- Font atlases blur during zoom operations
- Hit-testing for drag-and-drop is complex in WebGL
- Text rendering feels less crisp than native canvas/DOM

By switching to Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D) at tactical zoom levels, we get:
- Crisp text rendering at all scales
- Native canvas hit-testing for smooth drag-and-drop
- Better performance for hundreds of labeled PM blocks

   247→
   248→```typescript
   249→interface CanvasLayers {
   250→  // === DECK.GL LAYERS (Strategic/Overview) ===
   251→  // Used for zoom levels 0-8 where text is minimal
   252→  
   253→  strategicHeatmapLayer: HeatmapLayer;    // PM density heatmap
   254→  timelineAxisLayer: LineLayer;            // Time axis lines
   255→  quarterBoundaryLayer: LineLayer;         // Quarter separators
   256→  
   257→  // === KONVA LAYERS (Tactical/Operational) ===
   258→  // Used for zoom levels 8-16 where text labels are critical
   259→  
   260→  ganttBlockLayer: Konva.Layer;            // PM blocks with labels
   261→  constraintWallLayer: Konva.Layer;        // Compliance windows
   262→  trainLabelLayer: Konva.Layer;            // Train number labels
   263→  tooltipLayer: Konva.Layer;               // Hover tooltips
   264→  dragPreviewLayer: Konva.Layer;           // Drag-and-drop preview
   265→  
   266→  // === REACT DOM OVERLAY (Detail) ===
   267→  // Used for zoom level 16+ where rich interactions needed
   268→  
   269→  detailCardOverlay: React.ReactNode;      // Expanded PM cards
   270→  editFormOverlay: React.ReactNode;        // Inline editing
   271→}
   272→
   273→// Layer visibility by zoom level
   274→function getActiveRenderer(zoom: number): 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react' {
   275→  if (zoom < 8) return 'deckgl';
   276→  if (zoom < 16) return 'konva';
   277→  return 'react';
   278→}
   279→```
   280→
   281→**Transition Handling:**
   282→
   283→When crossing renderer boundaries, animate opacity to avoid jarring switches:
   284→
   285→```typescript
   286→function handleZoomChange(prevZoom: number, newZoom: number): void {
   287→  const prevRenderer = getActiveRenderer(prevZoom);
   288→  const newRenderer = getActiveRenderer(newZoom);
   289→  
   290→  if (prevRenderer !== newRenderer) {
   291→    // Cross-fade over 200ms
   292→    animateOpacity(prevRenderer, 1, 0, 200);
   293→    animateOpacity(newRenderer, 0, 1, 200);
   294→  }
   295→}
   296→```

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. ## 6.9 Accessibility Requirements

### 6.9.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Keyboard navigation | Full canvas navigation via arrow keys, Tab for controls |
| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all interactive elements |
| Color contrast | 4.5:1 minimum for text, 3:1 for large text |
| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on all interactive elements |
| Alternative text | All icons have text alternatives |
| Reduced motion | Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` setting |

### 6.9.2 Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | Navigate between panels |
| `Arrow keys` | Pan canvas |
| `+` / `-` | Zoom in/out |
| `Home` | Go to today |
| `1-5` | Switch zoom levels |
| `Escape` | Cancel drag / Close dialog |
| `Enter` | Activate selected element |
| `Space` | Toggle selection |
| `?` | Show keyboard shortcuts |

---

## 6.10 Responsive Behavior

### 6.10.1 Breakpoints

| Breakpoint | Width | Layout Changes |
|------------|-------|----------------|
| Desktop XL | â‰¥1920px | Full layout with all panels |
| Desktop | â‰¥1440px | Standard layout |
| Desktop SM | â‰¥1280px | Collapsible side panels |
| Tablet | â‰¥1024px | Stacked panels, simplified canvas |
| Mobile | <1024px | Not supported (show message) |

```typescript
const PERFORMANCE_OPTIMIZATIONS = {
  // Data loading
  virtualScrolling: true,           // Only render visible train rows
  tileBasedLoading: true,           // Load canvas data in tiles
  progressiveLoading: true,         // Load coarse data first, refine
  
  // Rendering
  webWorkerCalculations: true,      // Offload heavy math to workers
  gpuAcceleration: true,            // Use WebGL via deck.gl
  memoizedFiltering: true,          // Cache filtered datasets
  levelOfDetail: true,              // Reduce detail at low zoom
  
  // Caching
  apiResponseCache: '5m',           // Cache API responses
  canvasDataCache: '1m',            // Cache canvas tile data
  userPreferencesCache: 'session',  // Cache user settings
};
```

1→# MMS Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→> **Status**: Comprehensive plan generated from spec analysis (v2.1)
     4→> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-19
     5→> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) → P2 (High) → P3 (Medium) → P4 (Low)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Current State Analysis
    10→
    11→**Codebase Status**: Database Layer Complete (Phase 1.1)
    12→- React 18 + Vite frontend shell (App.tsx, main.tsx)
    13→- **Database schema**: 30+ tables across 14 schema files (Drizzle ORM)
    14→  - Core Fleet: depots, fleets, trains, routes, tracks
    15→  - Operational Constraints: service_demand, route_utilization_profiles, unavailability_events, train_route_compatibility
    16→  - PM Management: pm_types, pm_material_requirements, pm_schedule
    17→  - Mileage Tracking: mileage_readings, meter_events
    18→  - Component Tracking: train_components, component_installations, component_pm_tracking
    19→  - Material Management: inventory_positions, material_reservations
    20→  - Man-hour Planning: manhour_capacity, manhour_projections, manhour_adjustments
    21→  - Baseline & Drift: plan_baselines, baseline_pm_schedule, baseline_mileage_projection, drift_analysis
    22→  - Scenarios: scenarios, scenario_deltas, scenario_comparisons
    23→  - Costing: labor_rates, material_costs, cost_baselines
    24→  - Audit & Auth: users, roles, sessions, pm_schedule_history, audit_log
    25→- **Database utilities**: Connection pool with health checks (src/lib/db/connection.ts)
    26→- **Drizzle config**: Migration configuration (drizzle.config.ts)
    27→- **Manual migrations** (drizzle/manual/):
    28→  - PostgreSQL extensions (uuid-ossp, timescaledb)
    29→  - TimescaleDB hypertable (7-day chunks), compression (30 days), retention (13 years)
    30→  - daily_fleet_mileage continuous aggregate
    31→  - Database functions: train fitness sync, canvas refresh, scenario cleanup, reservation release, fleet targets, PM hierarchy, audit
    32→  - Canvas views: v_canvas_strategic, v_canvas_tactical, v_manhour_canvas, v_drift_canvas
    33→  - Development seed data
    34→- Dependencies: drizzle-orm, postgres.js, pino, @belt/tools
    35→
    36→**Next Priority**: Core Domain Services (Ingestion, Unavailability, Service Demand, Fleet Service)
    37→
    38→**Critical Path**: ~~Database Schema~~ → ~~TimescaleDB Config~~ → ~~Shared Lib~~ → Domain Services → Optimizer → API → UI
    39→
    40→---
    41→
    42→## Phase 1: Foundation (P1 - Critical)
    43→
    44→### 1.1 Database Schema & Migrations
    45→
    46→#### Core Fleet Domain
    47→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `depots` table (code, name, location, timezone)

    49→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `trains` table (train_number, fleet_id, current_mileage_km, fitness_status, target_mileage_km, display_color)
    50→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `routes` table (route_code, expected_km_per_day, required_trains, allowed_fleet_ids, signaling_system, power_system)
    51→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `tracks` table (depot_id, track_number, track_type, max_train_length_m, capabilities)
    52→
    53→#### Operational Constraints Domain (v2.1 Critical)
    54→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `service_demand` table (fleet_id, day_type, min_trains_required, peak requirements)
    55→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `route_utilization_profiles` table (route_id, weekday_km, weekend_km, confidence)
    56→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `unavailability_events` table (train_id, event_type, status, restriction_level, estimated_return_date, source_system)
    57→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `train_route_compatibility` table (train_id/fleet_id, route_id, is_compatible, constraint_type)
    58→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `track_capabilities` table (track_id, capability, is_operational)
    59→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_capability_requirements` table (pm_type_id, capability, is_mandatory)
    60→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_baseline_assumptions` table (train_id, pm_type_id, assumption_type, confidence_level) - cold start handling
    61→
    62→#### PM Management Domain
    63→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_types` table (fleet_id, pm_code, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes[], estimated_hours, track_type)
    64→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_material_requirements` table (pm_type_id, part_number, quantity_per_pm, criticality, lead_time_days)
    65→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_schedule` table (train_id, pm_type_id, target_date, status, constraint_start/end, assigned_track_id, is_locked, scenario_id)
    66→

    68→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `mileage_readings` hypertable (train_id, reading_date, raw_reading, adjusted_reading, daily_km, validation_status)

    79→

    87→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `manhour_adjustments` table (projection_id, adjustment_type, hours_adjusted, status)
    88→
    89→#### Baseline & Drift Domain
    90→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `plan_baselines` table (name, version, baseline_date, status, pm metrics)
    91→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `baseline_pm_schedule` table (baseline_id, train_id, pm_type_id, projected_date)
    92→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `baseline_mileage_projection` table (baseline_id, train_id, projection_date, projected_cumulative_km)
    93→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `drift_analysis` table (baseline_id, train_id, mileage_drift_km, drift_pct, requires_replan)
    94→
    95→#### Scenario Management (Event-Sourced)
    96→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `scenarios` table (name, forked_from_baseline_id, status, expires_at)
    97→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `scenario_deltas` table (scenario_id, sequence_num, delta_type, entity_id, old_values, new_values)
    98→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `scenario_comparisons` table (scenario_a_id, scenario_b_id, baseline_id, metrics)
    99→
   100→#### Costing Domain
   101→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `labor_rates` table (depot_id, regular_hourly_rate, overtime_multiplier, shop_rate)
   102→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `material_costs` table (part_number, unit_cost, effective_from)
   103→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `cost_baselines` table (fiscal_year, depot_id, budget vs. actual)
   104→
   105→#### Audit & Auth Domain
   106→- [x] P1: Create Drizzle schema for `pm_schedule_history` table (pm_schedule_id, change_type, old/new values, changed_by)

   111→
   112→#### Enums & Types
   113→- [x] P1: Create PostgreSQL enums: fitness_status, pm_status, track_type, shift_type, adjustment_status, baseline_status, scenario_status, material_criticality, reservation_type, day_type, unavailability_type, unavailability_status, track_capability, constraint_type, assumption_type, confidence_level

   116→- [x] P1: Create trigger `sync_train_fitness_from_unavailability()` - auto-update train fitness on unavailability events

   190→#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI)
   191→- [ ] P2: Create src/store/index.ts with @belt/tools createMachine
   192→- [ ] P2: Create fleetSlice (trains, selectedTrain, fitnessStatus filters)
   193→- [ ] P2: Create canvasSlice (zoom, pan, viewportBounds, activeLayer)

   195→- [ ] P2: Create batch dispatch utility for 60fps WebGL updates (requestAnimationFrame batching)
   196→
   197→#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
   198→```typescript
   199→// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
   200→import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';
   201→
   202→const TrainModel = createSchema({
   203→  id: 'string',
   204→  trainNumber: 'string',
   205→  mileageKm: 'number',
   206→  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
   207→  tags: 'string[]',
   208→  metadata: 'object?',
   209→});
   210→type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
   211→const train = TrainModel.parse(data);
   212→
   213→// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
   214→import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';
   215→
   216→const fleetSlice = createSlice({
   217→  id: 'fleet',
   218→  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
   219→  cache: { policy: 'session' },
   220→  reducers: {
   221→    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
   222→    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
   223→  },
   224→});
   225→
   226→const machine = createMachine({
   227→  id: 'mms',
   228→  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
   229→  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
   230→});
   231→
   232→// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
   233→const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
   234→```

# MMS Implementation Plan

> **Status**: Comprehensive plan generated from spec analysis (v2.1)
> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-19
> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) → P2 (High) → P3 (Medium) → P4 (Low)

---

## Current State Analysis

**Codebase Status**: Database Layer Complete (Phase 1.1)
- React 18 + Vite frontend shell (App.tsx, main.tsx)
- **Database schema**: 30+ tables across 14 schema files (Drizzle ORM)
  - Core Fleet: depots, fleets, trains, routes, tracks
  - Operational Constraints: service_demand, route_utilization_profiles, unavailability_events, train_route_compatibility
  - PM Management: pm_types, pm_material_requirements, pm_schedule
  - Mileage Tracking: mileage_readings, meter_events
  - Component Tracking: train_components, component_installations, component_pm_tracking
  - Material Management: inventory_positions, material_reservations
  - Man-hour Planning: manhour_capacity, manhour_projections, manhour_adjustments
  - Baseline & Drift: plan_baselines, baseline_pm_schedule, baseline_mileage_projection, drift_analysis
  - Scenarios: scenarios, scenario_deltas, scenario_comparisons
  - Costing: labor_rates, material_costs, cost_baselines
  - Audit & Auth: users, roles, sessions, pm_schedule_history, audit_log

> React hooks (useState, useEffect, etc.) are BLOCKED by .agentrc.ts - use machine.useMachine()

#### Schema Migration (Zod → @belt/tools)
- [ ] P1: Migrate primitive schemas (uuidSchema, dateStringSchema, mileageKmSchema) to createType
- [ ] P1: Migrate enum schemas (fitnessStatusSchema, pmStatusSchema, dayTypeSchema, etc.) to createType enum syntax
- [ ] P1: Migrate object schemas (trainSchema, pmScheduleSchema, mileageReadingSchema) to createSchema
- [ ] P1: Migrate CSV row schemas (mileageCsvRowSchema, fleetRosterCsvRowSchema) with transform handling
- [ ] P1: Migrate optimizer IPC schemas (optimizerInputSchema, optimizerOutputSchema)
- [ ] P1: Migrate API response schemas (apiSuccessSchema, apiErrorSchema)
- [ ] P1: Update parseSchema helper to use @belt/tools parse method
- [ ] P1: Update formatZodErrors to work with @belt/tools validation errors
- [ ] P1: Update all service imports from 'zod' to '@belt/tools'
- [ ] P1: Remove 'zod' from package.json dependencies
- [ ] P1: Remove 'zustand' from package.json dependencies (FORBIDDEN by hooks)

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI)
- [ ] P2: Create src/store/index.ts with @belt/tools createMachine
- [ ] P2: Create fleetSlice (trains, selectedTrain, fitnessStatus filters)
- [ ] P2: Create canvasSlice (zoom, pan, viewportBounds, activeLayer)
- [ ] P2: Create pmSlice (schedules, selectedPM, dragState, constraints)
- [ ] P2: Create batch dispatch utility for 60fps WebGL updates (requestAnimationFrame batching)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

## Critical Dependencies

| Dependency | Source | Required For | Fallback |
|-----------|--------|--------------|----------|
| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | PM scheduling validity | Manual status UI |
| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Material constraint validation | Assume unlimited (warning flag) |
| Service demand | Operations input | Run assignment optimization | Default 85% fleet |
| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Mileage projections | Fleet-wide average (warning flag) |

---

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have comprehensive understanding of the spec and current state. Let me compile the detailed extraction report.

## Comprehensive UI Specification Extraction Report

### Overview
The MMS project has **spec/006-user-interface.md** (26,771 tokens) that comprehensively defines a sophisticated infinite canvas interface using hybrid rendering (deck.gl + Konva + React DOM). Current implementation status is minimal: only barebones React 18 + Vite shell with BlueprintJS dependency installed.

---

## 1. INFINITE CANVAS ARCHITECTURE

### 1.1 Hybrid Rendering Strategy (CRITICAL)

The spec mandates **three distinct renderers based on semantic zoom levels**:

| Zoom Level | Range | Renderer | Data Granularity | Use Case |
|-----------|-------|----------|------------------|----------|
| Strategic | 0-4 | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Yearly PM aggregates | 12-year fleet overview |
| Tactical-Wide | 4-8 | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Monthly compressed blocks | 1-year planning |
| Tactical | 8-12 | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D)** | Weekly Gantt bars | 12-week detailed planning |
| Operational | 12-16 | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D)** | Daily cards with labels | 4-week resource detail |
| Detail | 16-20 | **React DOM** | Daily with crew assignment | 1-week expanded cards |

**Why Hybrid?** (spec 6.3.5)
- deck.gl WebGL limitations: Font atlases blur during zoom, complex hit-testing for drag-and-drop, crisp text rendering lacking
- Konva benefits: Native HTML5 Canvas 2D rendering with crisp text at all scales, smooth drag-and-drop via native hit-testing
- React DOM enables rich interactions (forms, buttons, nested lists) at maximum detail

**Transition Logic** (spec 6.3.5):
```typescript
function getActiveRenderer(zoom: number): 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react' {
  if (zoom < 8) return 'deckgl';
  if (zoom < 16) return 'konva';
  return 'react';
}
// Cross-fade opacity over 200ms when renderer changes
```

### 1.2 Canvas Layers Structure (spec 6.3.5)

```typescript
interface CanvasLayers {
  // deck.gl layers (zoom 0-8)
  strategicHeatmapLayer: HeatmapLayer;    // PM density
  timelineAxisLayer: LineLayer;            // Time axis
  quarterBoundaryLayer: LineLayer;         // Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4 markers

  // Konva layers (zoom 8-16)
  ganttBlockLayer: Konva.Layer;            // PM blocks with labels
  constraintWallLayer: Konva.Layer;        // Compliance window boundaries
  trainLabelLayer: Konva.Layer;            // Train number labels
  tooltipLayer: Konva.Layer;               // Hover tooltips
  dragPreviewLayer: Konva.Layer;           // Drag ghost preview

  // React DOM overlay (zoom 16+)
  detailCardOverlay: React.ReactNode;      // Rich PM cards
  editFormOverlay: React.ReactNode;        // Inline editing forms
}
```

---

## 2. SEMANTIC ZOOM LEVELS & TRANSITIONS

### 2.1 Strategic View (Heatmap) - Zoom 0-4

**Representation**: 12-year heatmap with yearly cells per train
- X-axis: Years 2026-2038
- Y-axis: Train list (all 128 trains)
- Cell colors indicate PM density:
  - `▓▓▓▓` = High (4+ PMs/year)
  - `▓▓░░` = Medium (2-3 PMs/year)
  - `░░░░` = Low (0-1 PMs/year)

**Interactions**:
- Hover: Tooltip showing PM count, total hours, cost estimate
- Click: Zoom into that year (transition to tactical-wide)

**Data Source**: `v_canvas_strategic` materialized view (aggregated yearly PM counts)

### 2.2 Tactical-Wide View - Zoom 4-8

**Representation**: 1-year timeline with monthly compressed Gantt blocks
- Month-wide blocks (not week-wide)
- Minimal text labels (space constrained)
- Block colors per PM status (see color system below)

**Interactions**:
- Hover: Show week within month
- Click: Zoom to specific week (transition to tactical)

**Data Source**: `v_canvas_tactical` materialized view

### 2.3 Tactical View (Gantt) - Zoom 8-12

**Representation**: 12-week Gantt with full labels
- X-axis: Weeks (Jan W1, Jan W2, Feb W1, etc.)
- Y-axis: Trains with status indicators (FIT, RESTRICTED, GROUNDED)
- PM blocks with:
  - Duration (days)
  - PM type label (C1, C2, C3, C4)
  - Track assignment (T1, T2, T3, Heavy)
  - Lock indicator (🔒 = locked, cannot move)

**Constraint Visualization**:
- Vertical "constraint walls" at compliance window boundaries:
  - ⅄ = Earliest allowed date (10% early tolerance)
  - ⅄ = Latest allowed date (10% late tolerance, RED if close)

**Interactions**:
- Hover PM block: Shows full details tooltip
- Drag PM block: Enables rescheduling (see section 4 below)

**Data Source**: `v_canvas_tactical` materialized view

### 2.4 Operational View (Detail Cards) - Zoom 12-16

**Representation**: 4-week timeline with expanded card detail for each PM
- Each PM is a full card showing:
  - Train number + PM type
  - Scheduled date range (Feb 15-17, 2026)
  - Status (SCHEDULED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED, DEFERRED)
  - Track assignment + depot
  - Estimated man-hours
  - Shift type (day/night)
  - Compliance window visualization (progress bar 0-100%)
  - Material availability:
    - ✓ Brake Pad Set (8 available)
    - ⚠ Bogie Seal Kit (2 available, 4 needed, arriving Jan 20)
  - Action buttons: [Edit] [Lock] [View History]

**Data Source**: `v_canvas_tactical` + detail expansion + `inventory_positions` join

### 2.5 Detail View - Zoom 16-20

**Representation**: 1-week view with crew assignment capability
- PM cards expanded with:
  - Crew assignments (technician names, shift times)
  - Material pickup confirmation
  - Sub-task breakdown (if applicable)
  - Related PM history
  - Estimated vs. actual hours (in-progress)

**Not yet specified in detail** - planned for P3

---

## 3. ALL UI COMPONENTS REQUIRED

### 3.1 Layout Components

**Header Section** (always visible):
- Logo + "MMS" branding
- Fleet filter: [All ▼] with dropdown (KS, ALSTOM)
- Depot filter: [Bishan ▼] with dropdown
- Search bar (trains, PMs, dates)
- User menu (profile, settings, logout)

**Toolbar Section**:
- View mode buttons: [Strategic] [Tactical] [Operational] (tabs)
- Date navigation: [Today] [◀] [▶] with date display
- Overlays menu: [≡] dropdown with:
  - [x] Man-hour Utilization
  - [x] Baseline Drift Analysis
  - [x] Scenario Comparison
- Actions: [Publish Baseline] [Create Scenario] [Run Optimizer] [Export] [Help]

**Side Panels**:
- **Train List Panel** (left):
  - Scrollable list of trains with:
    - Train number (1001)
    - Fleet identifier (KS, ALSTOM)
    - Fitness status indicator (▲ = RESTRICTED, ● = FIT, ✕ = GROUNDED)
    - Current mileage (245,000 km)
  - Selection highlight
  - Fitness filter: [Filters ▼]

- **Minimap** (bottom-left):
  - Small overview of entire 12-year timeline
  - Viewport indicator box showing current view region
  - Zoomable/pannable

- **Man-hour Overlay Panel** (right, collapsible):
  - Week-by-week utilization table:
    - Week | Required | Available | Util% | Status
    - W1: 280h / 384h = 73% ▢▢▢▢▢▢▢▢▢▢▢▢ Under
    - W2: 320h / 384h = 83% ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓░░ OK
    - W3: 420h / 384h = 109% ▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓▓ OVER
  - Proposed adjustments sub-panel:
    - ⚡ Defer 36h from Feb 03 → Feb 17
    - ⚡ Add 20h overtime in Feb 03
    - [Approve] [Reject] [Details] buttons per adjustment
  - [Apply All Approved] [Regenerate Suggestions] buttons

**Status Bar** (bottom, persistent):
- Baseline info: "Baseline: Q1 2026 v3.2"
- Drift alert: "Drift: 3 trains need attention"
- Last sync timestamp: "Last sync: 10:30"

### 3.2 Canvas Overlay Panels

**Drift Analysis Overlay** (collapsible right panel):
- Title: "BASELINE DRIFT ANALYSIS"
- Comparing baseline: "Q1 2026 Baseline v3.2" (with date picker)
- Fleet summary box:
  - Total trains: 128
  - With drift: 45 (35%)
  - Requiring replan: 3 (2%) ⚠
  - Avg mileage drift: +1.2%
- Trains requiring attention (sorted by severity):
  - 🔴 Train 1005: +6.2% drift, +18 days schedule shift
    - Recommendation: Advance next C2 by 2 weeks
    - [View Details] [Create Adjustment]
  - 🔴 Train 1023: -5.8% drift, -12 days shift
  - 🟡 Train 1047: +4.1% drift (approaching)
- Baseline shadow layer on canvas (semi-transparent blocks showing baseline positions)
- Action buttons: [Show on Canvas] [Export Report] [Trigger Replan]

**Baseline Shadow Layer** (visual):
- When drift overlay active, canvas shows "shadow" PM blocks
- Semi-transparent, lighter color than actual positions
- Deviation arrows showing drift direction (+3 days, -5 days, etc.)

**Man-hour Utilization Overlay** (detailed above in side panel)

**Scenario Comparison View** (side-by-side):
- Left panel: "BASELINE: Q1 2026 v3.2" (Gantt)
- Right panel: "SCENARIO: Track Closure Analysis" (Gantt)
- Changes highlighted with arrows
- Differences summary:
  - PMs moved: 8
  - Avg shift: +12 days
  - Man-hours impact: +240h overtime
  - Cost impact: +$18,000
  - Overloaded weeks: +2 (Mar W1, Mar W2)
- Buttons: [View Detailed Diff] [Promote Scenario] [Discard Scenario]

### 3.3 Explanation Panels

**Run Assignment Explanation** (appears when selecting today's run assignment):
- Title: "RUN ASSIGNMENT EXPLANATION"
- Date + Depot: "Jan 15, 2026 | Depot: Bishan"
- Selected train → assigned route: "Train 1001 → Route NSL"
- Assignment score: 85/100
- Factors breakdown (collapsible):
  - ✓ MILEAGE_BALANCE: +50 pts (train 3,000km under target, NSL route 280km helps)
  - ✓ ROUTE_COMPATIBILITY: +20 pts (train certified for NSL)
  - ✓ DEPOT_PROXIMITY: +15 pts (train at Bishan depot)
- Alternatives considered (rejected):
  - Train 1005: Blocked (PM C2 due today)
  - Train 1008: Lower score 62 (over target mileage)
  - Train 1012: Blocked (Restricted fitness status)
- Buttons: [Override Assignment] [View All Assignments]

**PM Scheduling Explanation** (appears when selecting a scheduled PM):
- Title: "PM SCHEDULING EXPLANATION"
- Train 1001 | C2 Inspection | Feb 15, 2026
- Why this date? (collapsible sections):
  - Mileage Trigger:
    - Current: 248,500 km
    - Threshold: 250,000 km (C2 interval)
    - Projected to reach: Feb 12, 2026
  - Compliance Window:
    - Earliest: Feb 1, 2026 (10% early tolerance)
    - Latest: Feb 28, 2026 (10% late tolerance)
    - Selected: Feb 15 (mid-window, best resource fit)
  - Resource Availability:
    - Track T1: Available Feb 15-17 ✓
    - Man-hours: 24h fits in Week 7 capacity ✓
    - Materials: All parts available ✓
  - Optimization Factors:
    - Grouped with Train 1002 C1 for efficiency
    - Avoids Week 6 overload (moved from Feb 10)
    - Balances depot workload across February
- Buttons: [View History] [Request Reschedule] [Lock Date]

### 3.4 Modals & Dialogs

**Import Modal** (CSV drag-and-drop):
- Drag zone: "Drop mileage CSV file here"
- Browse button: [Choose File]
- Progress indicator during upload
- Results summary: "123 records ingested, 2 anomalies detected"

**PM Move Confirmation** (appears after drag-drop release):
- Title: "Confirm PM Reschedule"
- "Move C2 for Train 1001 from Feb 15 to Feb 20?"
- Constraint impact display (if any):
  - ⚠ Warning: Week 8 will be 102% utilized (slight overload)
  - ❌ Error: Track T1 occupied by Train 1005 C3 (cannot proceed)
- Buttons: [Cancel] [Confirm]

**Edit PM Details Modal**:
- Train selector
- PM type selector (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul)
- Target date picker
- Track selector (with availability check)
- Shift selector (day/night)
- Material reservations section
- Lock toggle
- Buttons: [Cancel] [Save] [Save & Schedule Next]

**Scenario Create/Edit Modal**:
- Scenario name input
- Fork from: [Baseline v3.2 ▼] or [Scenario X ▼]
- Parameters to modify:
  - [Add Parameter] button
  - PM_MOVE: Select PM + new date
  - PM_ADD: Select train + PM type + target date
  - PM_REMOVE: Select PM to remove
- Buttons: [Cancel] [Create] [Create & Run Comparison]

### 3.5 Notification Components

**Toast Notifications** (top-right stack):
```typescript
type ToastType = 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'info';

Examples:
- success: "PM Rescheduled - Train 1001 C2 moved from Feb 15 to Feb 20"
- error: "Cannot Move PM - Track T1 is occupied by another PM on this date"
- warning: "Drift Alert - 3 trains require replanning attention"
- info: "Optimization Complete - 45 trains assigned, 3 unassigned" (persistent, with action)
```

**Alert Banner** (top of page):
- Style: Yellow/orange background for warnings, red for critical
- Example: "⚠️ BASELINE DRIFT ALERT: 3 trains have exceeded drift threshold. Review recommended before EOD. [View] [✕]"
- Persistent until dismissed

---

## 4. DRAG-AND-DROP RESCHEDULING REQUIREMENTS

### 4.1 Complete Interaction Flow

```
Step 1: Hover over PM block
  → Cursor changes to grab hand
  → Block highlights (slight shadow/glow)
  → Tooltip appears (PM details: Train 1001 C2, Feb 15-17, 24h)

Step 2: Mouse down on PM block
  → Block enters "dragging" state (slightly raised)
  → Constraint walls become prominent (brighter lines)
  → Other PMs fade slightly (opacity 0.5)
  → Backdrop darker (focus effect)

Step 3: Drag PM to new position
  → Ghost preview follows cursor (semi-transparent)
  → Real-time validation happens continuously:
    • Drag into valid date range: Green outline on preview
    • Drag into constraint violation: Red outline + violation tooltip
  → Violation tooltip explains issue (floating, near cursor)

Step 4: Release over valid position
  → Confirmation dialog appears:
    Title: "Confirm PM Reschedule"
    Body: "Move C2 for Train 1001 from Feb 15 to Feb 20?"
    Impact display (if any):
      ⚠ Week 8 utilization will increase to 102%
      or
      ❌ Track T1 occupied by Train 1005 C3 (cannot proceed)
    Buttons: [Cancel] [Confirm]

Step 5a: User confirms
  → API call to PATCH /pm/schedule/:id
  → WebSocket broadcasts canvas:pm_updated to all clients
  → All connected clients update their canvases
  → History entry created (audit_log)
  → Success toast: "✓ PM Rescheduled - Train 1001 C2 moved"

Step 5b: Release over invalid position
  → PM snaps back to original position (smooth animation)
  → Error toast: "❌ Cannot Move PM - <violation reason>"
```

### 4.2 Constraint Violation Feedback (spec 6.4.2)

```typescript
interface DragValidationResult {
  valid: boolean;
  violations: {
    type: 'HARD' | 'SOFT';
    constraint: string;
    message: string;
    canOverride: boolean;  // Some SOFT constraints can be overridden
  }[];
  warnings: {
    type: string;
    message: string;
  }[];
  impacts: {
    manhourChange: number;      // Hours added/removed
    costChange: number;         // $ impact
    conflictsCreated: number;   // How many other PMs affected
  };
}

HARD Violations (cannot move):
- OUTSIDE_COMPLIANCE_WINDOW
  "Cannot schedule outside compliance window (Feb 1 - Feb 28)"
  
- TRACK_CONFLICT
  "Track T1 is already occupied by Train 1005 C3 on this date"
  
- MATERIAL_UNAVAILABLE
  "Required parts not available until Jan 20"
  
- PM_LOCKED
  "This PM is locked. Unlock it first to make changes."

SOFT Violations (can override with confirmation):
- OVERLOAD_WEEK
  "This will overload Week 7 by 24 hours (112% utilization)"
  canOverride: true (user can confirm in dialog)
```

### 4.3 Visual Feedback Details

**Valid Drop Indicator**:
- Green outline around preview (3px solid #10B981)
- Green checkmark icon ✓ near cursor
- "Valid position" tooltip

**Invalid Drop Indicator**:
- Red outline around preview (3px solid #EF4444)
- Red prohibition icon 🚫 near cursor
- Violation message tooltip (auto-positioning to avoid occlusion)

**Snap-back Animation**:
- Duration: 200ms ease-out
- Smooth curve back to original position

---

## 5. STATE MANAGEMENT APPROACH

### 5.1 CRITICAL: @belt/tools Machine (NOT zustand)

**Project Constraint**: 
- `zustand` is in package.json BUT **BLOCKED by `.claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh`**
- React hooks (`useState`, `useEffect`) are **BLOCKED by `.agentrc.ts`**
- **MANDATORY**: Use `@belt/tools createMachine()` (v0.1.2 in package.json)

### 5.2 Recommended Store Architecture (Phase 1.3 / Phase 7.4)

```typescript
// src/store/index.ts

import { createSlice, createMachine, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

// Slice 1: Fleet State
const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: {
    trains: [] as Train[],
    routes: [] as Route[],
    selectedTrain: null as string | null,
    fleetFilter: 'ALL',  // 'ALL' | 'KS' | 'ALSTOM'
  },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains: Train[]) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id: string) => { state.selectedTrain = id; },
    setFleetFilter: (state, filter: string) => { state.fleetFilter = filter; },
  },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
});

// Slice 2: Canvas State
const canvasSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'canvas',
  initial: {
    zoom: 8,              // 0-20
    pan: { x: 0, y: 0 }, // Pixel offsets
    viewportBounds: null as BoundingBox | null,
    zoomLevel: 'TACTICAL' as ZoomLevel,  // STRATEGIC, TACTICAL_WIDE, TACTICAL, OPERATIONAL, DETAIL
    activeRenderer: 'konva' as 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react',
    selectedPM: null as string | null,
    hoveredPM: null as string | null,
  },
  reducers: {
    setZoom: (state, zoom: number) => { 
      state.zoom = Math.max(0, Math.min(20, zoom)); 
      // Recalculate zoomLevel and activeRenderer
    },
    setPan: (state, pan: { x: number; y: number }) => { state.pan = pan; },
    selectPM: (state, id: string | null) => { state.selectedPM = id; },
    hoverPM: (state, id: string | null) => { state.hoveredPM = id; },
  },
  cache: { policy: 'memory' },  // Real-time, don't persist
});

// Slice 3: PM Schedule State
const pmSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'pm',
  initial: {
    schedules: [] as PMSchedule[],
    dragState: {
      isDragging: false,
      startDate: null as Date | null,
      endDate: null as Date | null,
      ghostPosition: null as { x: number; y: number } | null,
      validationResult: null as DragValidationResult | null,
    },
    constraints: {
      materialBlocks: [] as MaterialBlock[],
      trackConflicts: [] as TrackConflict[],
    },
  },
  reducers: {
    setSchedules: (state, schedules: PMSchedule[]) => { state.schedules = schedules; },
    startDrag: (state, { startDate, endDate }) => {
      state.dragState.isDragging = true;
      state.dragState.startDate = startDate;
      state.dragState.endDate = endDate;
    },
    updateDragPreview: (state, { ghostPosition, validationResult }) => {
      state.dragState.ghostPosition = ghostPosition;
      state.dragState.validationResult = validationResult;
    },
    endDrag: (state) => {
      state.dragState.isDragging = false;
      state.dragState.ghostPosition = null;
      state.dragState.validationResult = null;
    },
  },
});

// Main machine
const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms-ui',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice, canvas: canvasSlice, pm: pmSlice },
  trackMetadata: false,  // CRITICAL for 60fps canvas updates
});

// In React component:
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('canvas');
// No useState/useEffect - all via machine
```

### 5.3 WebGL Batch Dispatch for 60fps

**Challenge**: deck.gl and Konva rendering is GPU-intensive. State mutations should batch during render cycles.

```typescript
// src/store/batch.ts
let pendingUpdates: any[] = [];
let rafId: number | null = null;

export function batchDispatch(action: () => void) {
  action();  // Execute immediately
  
  if (!rafId) {
    rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => {
      // Triggers single re-render for all batched changes
      rafId = null;
    });
  }
}

// Usage in drag handler:
export function onPMDrag(pmId: string, newDate: Date) {
  batchDispatch(() => {
    actions.startDrag({ startDate: newDate, endDate: newDate });
    // Validation happens, no re-render until RAF fires
    batchDispatch(() => {
      validateConstraints(pmId, newDate);
    });
  });
}
```

---

## 6. ACCESSIBILITY REQUIREMENTS (WCAG 2.1 AA)

### 6.1 Keyboard Navigation

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | Navigate between panels (header → toolbar → canvas → overlays) |
| Arrow keys | Pan canvas (←/→ for time, ↑/↓ for trains) |
| `+` / `-` | Zoom in/out (increment by 1) |
| `Home` / `End` | Go to today / end of horizon |
| `1`-`5` | Jump to zoom level (1=Strategic, 2=TacticalWide, 3=Tactical, 4=Operational, 5=Detail) |
| `Escape` | Cancel drag / close dialog |
| `Enter` | Activate selected element (PM detail) |
| `Space` | Toggle selection on PM block |
| `?` | Show keyboard shortcuts help modal |
| `l` | Lock/unlock selected PM |
| `d` | Delete/cancel selected PM (with confirmation) |
| `Ctrl+S` | Save baseline (if admin) |

### 6.2 ARIA Labels & Roles

**Canvas Container**:
```html
<div role="region" aria-label="Fleet Schedule Canvas" aria-live="polite">
  <!-- Konva canvas or deck.gl -->
</div>
```

**PM Blocks** (interactive):
```html
<rect role="button" 
      aria-label="Train 1001 C2 Inspection, Feb 15-17, 24 hours, Track T1"
      aria-pressed="false"
      tabindex="0"
/>
```

**Overlays**:
```html
<aside role="complementary" aria-label="Man-hour Utilization Overlay">
  <!-- Content -->
</aside>
```

### 6.3 Color Contrast

- Text on background: 4.5:1 minimum (normal text)
- Large text (18pt+): 3:1 minimum
- All PM status colors have sufficient contrast with white/black text

**Color Palette** (verified contrast):
```typescript
const colors = {
  pm: {
    scheduled: '#3B82F6' + white text = 5.2:1 ✓
    inProgress: '#F59E0B' + dark text = 4.1:1 ✓
    completed: '#10B981' + white text = 4.8:1 ✓
    overdue: '#EF4444' + white text = 5.3:1 ✓
  }
}
```

### 6.4 Focus Indicators

- All interactive elements have visible focus ring (2px solid #3B82F6 or high-contrast)
- Focus trap in modals (Tab loops within modal only)
- Focus restoration when modal closes

### 6.5 Reduced Motion

```typescript
// Detect user preference
const prefersReducedMotion = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;

// Disable animations:
- Zoom transitions: 0ms instead of 200ms
- Pan smooth scroll: disabled, instant jumps
- Drag snap-back: instant instead of 200ms ease-out
- Tooltip fade: instant instead of 100ms
```

## 7. EXISTING VS. GAP ANALYSIS

### 7.1 Current Implementation Status

**IMPLEMENTED**:
- ✓ React 18 + Vite scaffold (App.tsx, main.tsx)
- ✓ BlueprintJS v6 dependency installed
- ✓ deck.gl v9.2.5 dependency installed
